enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
भारतीय įरज़वर् बैंक RESERVE BANK OF INDIA
RBI/FMRD/2024-25/117
FMRD.DIRD.01/14.01.023/2024-25 May 08, 2024 (Updated as on February 21, 2025)

To
All eligible market participants
Madam/Sir
Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Margining for Non-Centrally Cleared
OTC Derivatives) Directions, 2024
Please refer to Paragraph 10 of the Statement on Developmental and Regulatory
Policies announced as a part of the Bi-monthly Monetary Policy Statement for 2019-
20 dated February 06, 2020, on issuance of the Directions regarding exchange of
variation margin (VM) and initial margin (IM) for non-centrally cleared derivatives
(NCCDs).
enriched_requirements.object: on February 21, 2025) To All eligible market participants Madam/Sir Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Margining for Non-

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
FMRD.DIRD.02/14.01.023/2024-25 dated May 08, 2024
Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Margining for Non-Centrally Cleared
OTC Derivatives) Directions, 2024
(Updated as on February 21, 2025)
The Reserve Bank of India (hereinafter called the Reserve Bank) hereby issues the
following Directions, in exercise of the powers conferred under section 45W of the
Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934 (02 of 1934) read with section 45U of the Reserve
Bank of India Act, 1934 (02 of 1934) and the Foreign Exchange Management Act,
1999 (42 of 1999) and in supersession of Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India
(Variation Margin) Directions, 2022 dated June 01, 2022.
enriched_requirements.object: - Reserve Bank of India (Margining for Non-Centrally Cleared OTC Derivatives) Directions, 2024 (Updated as on February 21, 2

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
FEMA.25/RB-2000 dated
May 3, 2000), Foreign Exchange Management (Debt Instruments) Regulations, 2019
(Notification no.
enriched_requirements.object: Management (Debt Instruments) Regulations, 2019 (Notification no

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
06
dated May 08, 2024, as amended from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: from time to time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Short title and commencement
(1) These Directions shall be called the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India
(Margining for Non-Centrally Cleared OTC Derivatives) Directions, 2024.
enriched_requirements.object: Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Margining for Non-Centrally Cleared OTC Derivatives) Directions, 2024

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (2) These Directions shall come into force on November 08, 2024.
enriched_requirements.object: November 08, 2024

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Applicability
(1) The provisions of these Directions shall apply to the following contracts, which are
entered into on or after the date on which these Directions come into force:
enriched_requirements.object: contracts, which are entered into on or after the date on which these Directions come into force:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
FEMA 25/RB-2000 dated May
<image>
3, 2000) and Master Direction - Risk Management and Inter-Bank Dealings
dated July 05, 2016, as amended from time to time;
enriched_requirements.object: Master Direction - Risk Management and Inter-Bank Dealings dated July 05, 2016, as amended from time to time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(d) Any other non-centrally cleared derivative (NCCD) contract as may be
specified by the Reserve Bank.
enriched_requirements.object: Reserve Bank

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Contracts resulting from compression of grandfathered contracts together
with contracts which are subject to these Directions shall, however, be subject
to the margin requirements under these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: to the margin requirements under these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(a) Central counterparty means an entity that interposes itself between
counterparties to contracts traded in one or more financial markets, becoming
<image>
the buyer to every seller and the seller to every buyer and thereby ensuring
the performance of open contracts.
enriched_requirements.object: itself between counterparties to contracts traded in one or more financial markets, becoming <image> the buyer to every sell

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) Certificate of Deposit shall have the meaning assigned in paragraph 2(a)(iii)
of the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Certificate of Deposit)
Directions, 2021 dated June 04, 2021, as amended from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: in paragraph 2(a)(iii) of the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Certificate of Deposit) Directions, 2021 dated June

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(c) Commercial Paper shall have the meaning assigned in paragraph 2(a)(iv) of
the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Paper and Non-
Convertible Debentures of original or initial maturity upto one year) Directions,
2024 dated January 03, 2024, as amended from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: in paragraph 2(a)(iv) of the Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Commercial Paper and Non- Convertible Debentures of o

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(d) Consolidated group means a group within the meaning of Indian Accounting
Standard (Ind AS) 110 - Consolidated Financial Statements, or International
Financial Reporting Standards (IFRS) 10 - Consolidated Financial
Statements or any other equivalent accounting standards.
enriched_requirements.object: meaning of Indian Accounting Standard (Ind AS) 110 - Consolidated Financial Statements, or International Financial Reporting Standa

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(e) Collateralise to market means an approach to the exchange of Variation
Margin wherein the exchanged margin is characterised as collateral to secure
the current mark-to-market exposure between the parties to a derivative
contract.
enriched_requirements.object: exchange of Variation Margin wherein the exchanged margin is characterised as collateral to secure the current mark-to-market expos

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(g) Initial margin means the collateral that is collected to cover the potential
future exposure that could arise from future changes in the market value of a
derivative contract during the time it takes to close out and/or replace the
position in the event of a counterparty default.
enriched_requirements.object: collected to cover the potential future exposure that could arise from future changes in the market value of a derivative contr

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(h) Netting agreement shall have the same meaning as assigned to it in Section
2(1)(k) of The Bilateral Netting of Qualified Financial Contracts Act, 2020 (30
of 2020).
enriched_requirements.object: as assigned to it in Section 2(1)(k) of The Bilateral Netting of Qualified Financial Contracts Act, 2020 (30 of 2020)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(j) Non-resident means and includes a 'person resident outside India' as
defined in Section 2(w) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42
of 1999).
enriched_requirements.object: resident outside India' as defined in Section 2(w) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(k) Related parties shall have the same meaning as assigned to it under Indian
Accounting Standard (Ind AS) 24 - Related Party Disclosures or International
Accounting Standard (IAS) 24 - Related Party Disclosures or any other
equivalent accounting standards.
enriched_requirements.object: as assigned to it under Indian Accounting Standard (Ind AS) 24 - Related Party Disclosures or International Accounting Standard

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(l) Resident means and includes a 'person resident in India' as defined in
Section 2(v) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999).
enriched_requirements.object: resident in India' as defined in Section 2(v) of the Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999 (42 of 1999)

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(m) Settle to market means an approach to the exchange of Variation Margin
wherein the exchanged margin is deemed to settle the current mark-to-market
exposure between the parties to a derivative contract, with no right to reclaim
and no obligation to return the Variation Margin.
enriched_requirements.object: exchange of Variation Margin wherein the exchanged margin is deemed to settle the current mark-to-market exposure between the parti

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(n) Variation margin means the collateral that is collected or paid to reflect the
current mark-to-market exposure resulting from changes in the market value
of a derivative contract.
enriched_requirements.object: collected or paid to reflect the current mark-to-market exposure resulting from changes in the market value of a derivative con

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Words and expressions used but not defined in these Directions shall have the
meaning as assigned to them in the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934.
enriched_requirements.object: assigned to them in the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
4.1 Covered Entities for exchange of Variation Margin
(1) The following entities shall be classified as Domestic Covered Entities for
exchange of Variation Margin (hereafter known as Domestic Covered Entities -
VM) under these Directions:
enriched_requirements.object: Covered Entities for exchange of Variation Margin (hereafter known as Domestic Covered Entities - VM) under these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The following entities shall be classified as Foreign Covered Entities for exchange
of Variation Margin (hereafter known as Foreign Covered Entities - VM) under
these Directions:
<image>
enriched_requirements.object: Covered Entities for exchange of Variation Margin (hereafter known as Foreign Covered Entities - VM) under these Directions:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
4.2 Covered Entities for exchange of Initial Margin
(1) Entities regulated by a financial sector regulator (including branches of foreign
banks operating in India) and having an Average Aggregate Notional Amount (AANA)
of outstanding NCCDs of ₹60,000 crore and above, on a consolidated group wide
basis, shall be classified as Domestic Covered Entities for exchange of Initial Margin
(hereafter known as Domestic Covered Entities - IM) under these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: Covered Entities for exchange of Initial Margin (hereafter known as Domestic Covered Entities - IM) under these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Non-resident financial entities having an AANA of outstanding NCCDs of USD 8
billion and above, on a consolidated group wide basis, shall be classified as Foreign
Covered Entities for exchange of Initial Margin (hereafter known as Foreign Covered
Entities - IM) under these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: Covered Entities for exchange of Initial Margin (hereafter known as Foreign Covered Entities - IM) under these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
4.3 Average Aggregate Notional Amount of outstanding NCCDs
(1) AANA of outstanding NCCDs shall be calculated as the simple average of the total
notional amount of outstanding NCCDs as at the end of March, April and May of
a year.
enriched_requirements.object: simple average of the total notional amount of outstanding NCCDs as at the end of March, April and May of a year

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
AANA for a year shall be used for recognition of Domestic Covered Entities
and Foreign Covered Entities for a one-year period from September 1 of that year
to August 31 of the next year.
enriched_requirements.object: of Domestic Covered Entities and Foreign Covered Entities for a one-year period from September 1 of that year to August 31 of

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) AANA calculation shall include all NCCD contracts of the consolidated group,
including those outside the scope of these Directions, but exclude intra-group
transactions.
enriched_requirements.object: of the consolidated group, including those outside the scope of these Directions, but exclude intra-group transactions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
4.4 Directions for Covered Entities
(1) A Domestic Covered Entity - VM shall exchange Variation Margin with a
counterparty to an NCCD transaction if the counterparty is a Domestic Covered
Entity - VM or a Foreign Covered Entity - VM.
enriched_requirements.object: a counterparty to an NCCD transaction if the counterparty is a Domestic Covered Entity - VM or a Foreign Covered Entity

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) A Domestic Covered Entity - IM shall exchange Initial Margin with a counterparty
to an NCCD transaction if the counterparty is a Domestic Covered Entity - IM or
a Foreign Covered Entity - IM.
enriched_requirements.object: a counterparty to an NCCD transaction if the counterparty is a Domestic Covered Entity - IM or a Foreign Covered Entity -

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Covered Entities shall put in place appropriate processes for ascertaining whether
a counterparty to an NCCD transaction is a Covered Entity for exchange of
Variation Margin or Initial Margin.
enriched_requirements.object: processes for ascertaining whether a counterparty to an NCCD transaction is a Covered Entity for exchange of Variation Margin

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For this purpose, Covered Entities may, inter
alia, rely on a declaration from the counterparties.
enriched_requirements.object: on a declaration from the counterparties

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (4) Covered Entities may choose not to exchange:

All other
payments or cash flows that occur during the life of the swap shall be subject
to Initial Margin requirements.
enriched_requirements.object: Margin requirements

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Covered Entities shall however, develop appropriate risk management policies
and procedures to manage the risks associated with such transactions.2
(5) The provisions of these Directions shall not be applicable to an NCCD transaction
in which one of the counterparties is any of the following entities:
enriched_requirements.object: NCCD transaction in which one of the counterparties is any of the following entities:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
and
2 Covered Entities may refer to BCBS Supervisory Guidance for Managing Risks Associated with the Settlement of FX Transactions, February 2013: https://www.bis.org/publ/bcbs241.pdf for management of FX-settlement related risks.
enriched_requirements.object: Guidance for Managing Risks Associated with the Settlement of FX Transactions, February 2013: https://www

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) The provisions of these Directions shall not be applicable to an NCCD transaction
between entities belonging to the same consolidated group.
enriched_requirements.object: NCCD transaction between entities belonging to the same consolidated group

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) An entity, on being classified as a Covered Entity, shall be required to exchange
initial margin and variation margin with other covered entities, as applicable, in
terms of these Directions, only for NCCD transactions undertaken after
recognition of the entity as a covered entity.
enriched_requirements.object: initial margin and variation margin with other covered entities, as applicable, in terms of these Directions, only for NCCD tr

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(8) A Covered Entity, on ceasing to be classified as a Covered Entity, may choose,
not to exchange margin for its NCCD transactions, including outstanding NCCD
transactions, from the date on which it ceases to be recognised as a Covered
Entity, in terms of these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: margin for its NCCD transactions, including outstanding NCCD transactions, from the date on which it ceases to be recognised a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
5.1 Variation Margin
(1) Variation Margin shall fully collateralise to market or settle to market, the mark-to-
market exposure of an NCCD contract.
enriched_requirements.object: or settle to market, the mark-to- market exposure of an NCCD contract

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In the event that the exposures cannot be
marked-to-market, a pre-agreed alternative process or fallback mechanism, as set
out in the credit support annex, shall be used for the purpose of calculation of
Variation Margin.
enriched_requirements.object: purpose of calculation of Variation Margin

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (2) Variation Margin shall be calculated on a daily basis.
enriched_requirements.object: daily basis

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Variation Margin shall be calculated on an aggregate net basis, across all NCCD
contracts that are executed under a single, legally enforceable netting agreement.
enriched_requirements.object: aggregate net basis, across all NCCD contracts that are executed under a single, legally enforceable netting agreement

conditions: when trades are added to or subtracted from the portfolio
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
5.2 Initial Margin
(1) Initial margin shall be calculated at the outset of a transaction and thereafter on a
regular and consistent basis upon changes in the potential future exposure, including,
but not limited to, when trades are added to or subtracted from the portfolio, on
occurrence of a significant market disruption, and when the Initial Margin model (if
applicable) is recalibrated.
enriched_requirements.object: outset of a transaction and thereafter on a regular and consistent basis upon changes in the potential future exposure, including, but not limited to

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
3https://rbidocs.rbi.org.in/rdocs/notification/PDFs/12MCBASELIIICAPITALREGULATIONSED3EF388F75E48198 FF8328B36F43670.PDF
<image>
Provided that the initial margin shall be recalculated within a period not exceeding 10
working days.
enriched_requirements.object: period not exceeding 10 working days

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The amount of Initial Margin shall be calculated by reference to either:
a.
enriched_requirements.object: to either: a

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Covered Entities may choose between the standardised approach and the model
approach for calculating Initial Margin for all transactions within the same asset
class i.e.
enriched_requirements.object: approach and the model approach for calculating Initial Margin for all transactions within the same asset class i

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Foreign exchange and
interest rate derivatives contracts shall be considered as being part of a single
asset class for the purpose of these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: part of a single asset class for the purpose of these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
For each asset class, the selection
of a particular approach shall be made on a consistent basis and the reasons for
selecting the particular approach as well as reasons for change in approach,
where applicable, shall be documented.
enriched_requirements.object: consistent basis and the reasons for selecting the particular approach as well as reasons for change in approach

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) The Reserve Bank may direct a Covered Entity to calculate Initial Margin using
the standardised approach, if the Reserve Bank is satisfied that it is necessary or
expedient to do so.
enriched_requirements.object: to calculate Initial Margin using the standardised approach

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Exchange of margin
(1) Variation Margin shall be exchanged on an aggregate net basis, across all NCCD
contracts that are executed under a single, legally enforceable netting agreement.
enriched_requirements.object: aggregate net basis, across all NCCD contracts that are executed under a single, legally enforceable netting agreement

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Initial Margin shall be exchanged on a gross basis without any netting of Initial
Margin amounts owed by the two counterparties, across all NCCD contracts that
are executed under a single, legally enforceable netting agreement.
enriched_requirements.object: gross basis without any netting of Initial Margin amounts owed by the two counterparties, across all NCCD contracts that are execute

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) A threshold amount, not exceeding ₹450 crore4, may be applied for the exchange
of Initial Margin.
enriched_requirements.object: exchange of Initial Margin

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
This threshold shall be applied at the level of the consolidated
group based on all NCCD contracts outstanding between the two consolidated
groups.
enriched_requirements.object: level of the consolidated group based on all NCCD contracts outstanding between the two consolidated groups

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The difference between the total calculated Initial Margin amount and the
Initial Margin threshold shall be exchanged between the two consolidated groups
if the total Initial Margin amount exceeds the threshold.
enriched_requirements.object: two consolidated groups if the total Initial Margin amount exceeds the threshold

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(4) A minimum transfer amount (MTA), not exceeding ₹4.5 crore, may be applied for
the exchange of Variation Margin and Initial Margin combined.
enriched_requirements.object: exchange of Variation Margin and Initial Margin combined

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The covered
entities may mutually agree to set separate MTAs for Variation Margin and Initial
Margin, subject to the condition that when combined, the MTAs do not exceed
₹4.5 crore.
enriched_requirements.object: separate MTAs for Variation Margin and Initial Margin

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The entire margin amount shall be exchanged if the margin amount
exceeds the MTA.
enriched_requirements.object: margin amount exceeds the MTA

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) Variation Margin and Initial Margin shall be called and exchanged at the earliest
time possible after the transaction date ("T") or margin recalculation date ("R"), but
no later than three local business days from the transaction date ("T+3") or margin
recalculation date ("R+3").
enriched_requirements.object: at the earliest time possible after the transaction date ("T") or margin recalculation date ("R"), but no later than three loc

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Variation Margin and Initial Margin for an NCCD transaction between a Domestic
Covered Entity and a Foreign Covered Entity may be posted/collected either in
India or in an overseas jurisdiction, subject to the provisions of the A.P.
enriched_requirements.object: India or in an overseas jurisdiction

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: 06 dated May 08, 2024 on Margin for Derivative Contracts.
enriched_requirements.object: for Derivative Contracts

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Treatment of Variation Margin under collateralise to market approach
(1) Cash collateral received as Variation Margin by banks shall not be subject to the
provisions of Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Interest Rate on Deposits)
Directions, 2016, as amended from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: provisions of Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Interest Rate on Deposits) Directions, 2016, as amended from time to time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Cash collateral received as Variation Margin by Authorised Dealers shall not be
subject to the provisions under Paragraph 5 of Part C of the Master Direction -
Risk Management and Inter-Bank Dealings, 2016, as amended from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: provisions under Paragraph 5 of Part C of the Master Direction - Risk Management and Inter-Bank Dealings, 2016, as amended from time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Counterparties may pay interest on cash collateral received as Variation Margin,
in terms of the credit support annex.
enriched_requirements.object: collateral received as Variation Margin, in terms of the credit support annex

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Cash and non-cash collateral received as Variation Margin may be re-
hypothecated, re-pledged or re-used, in terms of the credit support annex.
enriched_requirements.object: or re-used, in terms of the credit support annex

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Treatment of Initial Margin
(1) Initial Margin collected from counterparties shall be maintained in a manner such
that:
enriched_requirements.object: manner such that:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The Covered Entity shall deal with the Initial Margin collected as belonging to that
counterparty who posted it.
enriched_requirements.object: Margin collected as belonging to that counterparty who posted it

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Initial Margin collected shall be segregated from the proprietary assets of the
collecting party by either placing the Initial Margin with a collateral service
provider, subject to conditions specified in Paragraph 9(2) and 9(4) of these
Directions or through other legally effective arrangements.
enriched_requirements.object: proprietary assets of the collecting party by either placing the Initial Margin with a collateral service provider

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
In addition, the
collecting party shall also provide the posting party with the option to segregate
the Initial Margin that it posts from the assets of all other counterparties.
enriched_requirements.object: party with the option to segregate the Initial Margin that it posts from the assets of all other counterparties

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Initial Margin collected shall not be re-hypothecated, re-pledged or re-used or
used as margin or guarantee for, or to secure any other transaction.
enriched_requirements.object: re-used or used as margin or guarantee for, or to secure any other transaction

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) Subject to the prior written consent of the posting party, Initial Margin collected in
cash may be reinvested in eligible collateral specified under Paragraph 10 of these
Directions provided that the re-invested Initial Margin (after application of the
relevant haircuts) meets the provisions set out in these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: (after application of the relevant haircuts) meets the provisions set out in these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) Interest earned from the Initial Margin collected shall accrue to the counterparty
who posted it, subject to the agreement between the covered entities.
enriched_requirements.object: who posted it

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The
Covered Entity shall ensure that the interest accrued to the counterparty are paid
to or held for the benefit of the counterparty, as the case may be.
enriched_requirements.object: accrued to the counterparty are paid to or held for the benefit of the counterparty, as the case may be

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) Cash collateral received as Initial Margin by banks shall not be subject to the
provisions of Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Interest Rate on Deposits)
Directions, 2016, as amended from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: provisions of Master Direction - Reserve Bank of India (Interest Rate on Deposits) Directions, 2016, as amended from time to time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(8) Cash collateral received as Initial Margin by Authorised Dealers shall not be
subject to the provisions under Paragraph 5 of Part C of the Master Direction -
Risk Management and Inter-Bank Dealings, 2016, as amended from time to time.
enriched_requirements.object: provisions under Paragraph 5 of Part C of the Master Direction - Risk Management and Inter-Bank Dealings, 2016, as amended from time

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Collateral Service Provider - Eligibility
(1) A covered entity may place Initial Margin in India or overseas, subject to the
provisions of these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: India or overseas

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(2) Scheduled Commercial Banks and Clearing Corporation of India Limited (CCIL)
shall be eligible to act as a collateral service provider for Initial Margin posted in
India.
enriched_requirements.object: as a collateral service provider for Initial Margin posted in India

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The collateral service provider shall ensure that the Initial Margin held by it on
behalf of the posting entity is segregated and not commingled with its proprietary
assets.
enriched_requirements.object: Margin held by it on behalf of the posting entity is segregated and not commingled with its proprietary assets

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The collateral service provider shall also provide the posting entity with
the option to segregate the Initial Margin that it posts from the assets of all other
posting entities.
enriched_requirements.object: entity with the option to segregate the Initial Margin that it posts from the assets of all other posting entities

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) A covered entity, choosing to use a collateral service provider in a foreign
jurisdiction, shall ensure that the collateral service provider is duly authorized to
provide such services, in terms of the regulations of the foreign jurisdiction.
enriched_requirements.object: service provider is duly authorized to provide such services, in terms of the regulations of the foreign jurisdiction

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) The Covered Entity shall satisfy itself of the ability of the collateral service
provider to safekeep the Initial Margin collected.
enriched_requirements.object: ability of the collateral service provider to safekeep the Initial Margin collected

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Eligible collateral and haircuts
(1) Variation Margin between two Domestic Covered Entities - VM shall be
exchanged using the following collateral types:
enriched_requirements.object: following collateral types:

conditions:
If different ratings are accorded
by two or more credit rating agencies
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If different ratings are accorded
by two or more credit rating agencies, then the lowest rating shall be
reckoned.

If
different ratings are accorded by two or more credit rating agencies, then the
lowest rating shall be reckoned.

(2) Initial Margin between two Domestic Covered Entities - IM shall be exchanged
using the following collateral types:
enriched_requirements.object: following collateral types:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) Variation Margin between a Domestic Covered Entity - VM and a Foreign
Covered Entity - VM shall be exchanged using the following collateral types,
subject to the provisions of the A.P.
enriched_requirements.object: following collateral types

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
06 dated May 08,
2024 on Margin for Derivative Contracts:
enriched_requirements.object: for Derivative Contracts:

conditions:
If different ratings are accorded by two or more
credit rating agencies
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
If different ratings are accorded by two or more
credit rating agencies, then the lowest rating shall be reckoned;

(4) Initial Margin between a Domestic Covered Entity - IM and a Foreign Covered
Entity - IM shall be exchanged using the following collateral types, subject to the
provisions of the A.P.
enriched_requirements.object: following collateral types

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
06 dated May 08, 2024 on Margin
for Derivative Contracts:
enriched_requirements.object: for Derivative Contracts:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: (5) Risk-sensitive haircuts shall be applied to the value of the collateral received.
enriched_requirements.object: value of the collateral received

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) For the purpose of meeting Variation Margin requirements, an additional currency
mismatch haircut of eight per cent shall be applied to all non-cash collateral
received in a currency other than the base currency or eligible currencies as
agreed in the credit support annex.
enriched_requirements.object: non-cash collateral received in a currency other than the base currency or eligible currencies as agreed in the credit support annex

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) For the purpose of meeting Initial Margin requirements, an additional currency
mismatch haircut of eight per cent shall be applied to all cash and non-cash
collateral received in a currency other than the termination currency that the
counterparty which has posted the collateral has designated in the relevant
contract.
enriched_requirements.object: cash and non-cash collateral received in a currency other than the termination currency that the counterparty which has posted the c

enriched_requirements.full_sentence: Each counterparty may specify one termination currency.

(8) Securities issued by either of the counterparties to an NCCD transaction, or their
related parties, shall not be accepted as collateral.

(9) Counterparties shall establish appropriate controls to manage the risks associated
with the collateral received including, inter-alia, wrong-way risk5, concentration
risk and liquidity risk.
enriched_requirements.object: manage the risks associated with the collateral received including, inter-alia, wrong-way risk5, concentration risk

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(10) Eligible collateral that was originally posted or collected may be substituted
provided that the substituted collateral meets all the requirements set out in these
Directions and the value of the substituted collateral, after the application of risk-
sensitive haircuts, is sufficient to meet the margin requirement
(11) A Domestic Covered Entity choosing to comply with margin requirements of a
foreign jurisdiction, in accordance with Paragraph 11 of these Directions, may
exchange margin using the collateral types, permitted in terms of the margin
requirements of the foreign jurisdiction.
enriched_requirements.object: all the requirements set out in these Directions and the value of the substituted collateral, after the application o

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Compliance with Margin Requirements of a Foreign Jurisdiction
(1) An NCCD transaction between a Domestic Covered Entity and a Foreign Covered
Entity may be subject to margin requirements in a foreign jurisdiction.
enriched_requirements.object: requirements in a foreign jurisdiction

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
A Domestic
Covered Entity and its counterparty in the foreign jurisdiction may decide to
comply with these Directions, or the margin requirements implemented by the
foreign jurisdiction provided the margining framework in the foreign jurisdiction is
assessed by the Domestic Covered Entity to be comparable to the requirements
in these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: these Directions, or the margin requirements implemented by the foreign jurisdiction provided the margining framework in the for

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) Two Domestic Covered Entities that are authorised to deal in foreign exchange
under Section 10(1) of FEMA,1999 and at least one of which is a branch of foreign
bank operating in India may decide to exchange initial margin for NCCD
transactions between them in accordance with these Directions or the margin
requirements of a foreign jurisdiction provided the margining framework in the
foreign jurisdiction is assessed by both the Domestic Covered Entities to be
comparable to the requirements in these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: margin for NCCD transactions between them in accordance with these Directions or the margin requirements of a foreign juris

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(3) The Domestic Covered Entity shall assess the comparability of the margining
framework of the foreign jurisdictions based on the following broad principles:
enriched_requirements.object: the margining framework of the foreign jurisdictions based on the following broad principles:

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) For this purpose, the Domestic Covered Entity shall put in place a Board-approved
policy for the comparability assessment.
enriched_requirements.object: Board-approved policy for the comparability assessment

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The assessment of the margining
framework of each foreign jurisdiction shall be placed before the Risk
Management Committee of the Board/ equivalent body and subject to periodic
review.
enriched_requirements.object: Risk Management Committee of the Board/ equivalent body and subject to periodic review

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) The Reserve Bank may, at a future date, undertake comparability assessment of
margining framework of foreign jurisdictions vis-à-vis these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: date, undertake comparability assessment of margining framework of foreign jurisdictions vis-à-vis these Directions

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such
6 Banks may refer to Annex 18 (part B) of Master Circular on Basel III Capital Regulations dated May 12, 2023 in this regard.
enriched_requirements.object: (part B) of Master Circular on Basel III Capital Regulations dated May 12, 2023 in this regard

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(6) The Reserve Bank, based on its assessment of the margining framework of a
foreign jurisdiction, may impose additional conditions to be met by the
counterparties intending to comply with the margining framework of that
jurisdiction.
enriched_requirements.object: be met by the counterparties intending to comply with the margining framework of that jurisdiction

conditions:
if there is significant doubt regarding the
enforceability of the close-out netting and collateral arrangements
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(7) A Domestic Covered Entity may not exchange margin in an NCCD transaction
with a Foreign Covered Entity if there is significant doubt regarding the
enforceability of the close-out netting and collateral arrangements, subject to the
following:
enriched_requirements.object: NCCD transaction with a Foreign Covered Entity if there is significant doubt regarding the enforceability of the close-out netting and collateral arrangements

conditions:
where close-out netting and/or collateral
arrangements are not legally enforceable
enriched_requirements.object: and document the basis for identifying a jurisdiction where close-out netting and/or collateral arrangements are not legally

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) For each jurisdiction so assessed, the Domestic Covered Entity shall put in
place appropriate internal limits and controls to manage its exposure to
counterparties located in the jurisdiction.
enriched_requirements.object: internal limits and controls to manage its exposure to counterparties located in the jurisdiction

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Dispute resolution
(1) Counterparties shall ensure that appropriate policies and procedures for dispute
resolution are in place before undertaking an NCCD transaction.
enriched_requirements.object: and procedures for dispute resolution are in place before undertaking an NCCD transaction

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Such policies
and procedures shall, inter-alia, include processes for determining discrepancies
in material terms or valuations as disputes, mechanism for such disputes to be
resolved and escalation of material disputes to senior management, or to the
Board, as may be appropriate.
enriched_requirements.object: for determining discrepancies in material terms or valuations as disputes, mechanism for such disputes to be resolved

conditions: In case of a margin dispute
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) In case of a margin dispute, counterparties shall exchange the non-disputed
amount first and make all necessary and appropriate efforts, including timely
initiation of dispute resolution protocols, to resolve the dispute and exchange the
remaining margin amount in a time-bound manner.
enriched_requirements.object: first and make all necessary and appropriate efforts, including timely initiation of dispute resolution protocols, to

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Obligation to provide information sought by the Reserve Bank
The Reserve Bank may call for any information or seek any clarification from any
agency involved in the NCCD markets, including but not limited to covered entities and
<image>
the collateral service provider, which in the opinion of the Reserve Bank is relevant
and the agency shall furnish such additional information and clarification within the
time frame specified.
enriched_requirements.object: and clarification within the time frame specified

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Annex I
Standardised approach for calculating Initial Margin
(1) For each netting agreement, the net standardised Initial Margin amount shall be
calculated as given below:
Table 1: Standardised Initial Margin Schedule
Asset Class Initial Margin Requirement
(% of Notional Exposure)
Foreign Exchange 6
Interest Rate Residual maturity: 0 - 2 year 1
Residual maturity: 2 - 5 years 2
Residual maturity: > 5 years 4
Credit Residual maturity: 0 - 2 year 2
Residual maturity: 2 - 5 years 5
Residual maturity: > 5 years 10
Others 15
enriched_requirements.object: below: Table 1: Standardised Initial Margin Schedule Asset Class Initial Margin Requirement (% of Notional Exposure) Foreign Ex

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) NCCD transactions for which an entity faces no counterparty risk may be excluded
from the Initial Margin calculation.
enriched_requirements.object: Initial Margin calculation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
Annex II
Model approach for calculating Initial Margin
(1) The quantitative portfolio margin model used to calculate Initial Margin shall meet
the following standards:

enriched_requirements.object: factors which materially influence the NCCD contracts and reflect the nature, scale and complexity of the risks inherent in
the potential future exposure of NCCD contracts, based on a one-tailed 99 per cent confidence interval over a 10-day time hor

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(c) The model shall be calibrated based on historical data of not more than five
years, which incorporates a period of significant financial stress.
enriched_requirements.object: historical data of not more than five years, which incorporates a period of significant financial stress

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The period
of financial stress shall be identified and applied separately for each asset
class.
enriched_requirements.object: separately for each asset class

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The data within the identified period shall be equally weighted for
calibration purposes;
enriched_requirements.object: calibration purposes

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(d) The model shall consider all NCCD contracts that are agreed for model use
and are subject to the same legally enforceable netting agreement;
enriched_requirements.object: that are agreed for model use and are subject to the same legally enforceable netting agreement

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(e) The model may account for diversification, hedging and risk offsets within
each asset class;
enriched_requirements.object: and risk offsets within each asset class

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(f) NCCD transactions for which an entity faces no counterparty risk may be
excluded from the Initial Margin calculation.
enriched_requirements.object: Initial Margin calculation

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(2) The quantitative portfolio margin model used to calculate Initial Margin shall be
subject to an internal governance process as given below:
enriched_requirements.object: internal governance process as given below:
and periodical (at least once a year) independent validation to validate its conceptual soundness and the appropriateness of the

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(b) The performance of the model shall be monitored on a continuous basis
including a regular back-testing programme;
enriched_requirements.object: continuous basis including a regular back-testing programme

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(c) The model shall be recalibrated at least once a year in accordance with
written recalibration policies.
enriched_requirements.object: once a year in accordance with written recalibration policies

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
<image>
(3) The model shall be approved by the Board of Directors (or equivalent forum) which
shall, inter alia, ensure ongoing compliance of the model with the provisions in
these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: Board of Directors (or equivalent forum) which shall, inter alia, ensure ongoing compliance of the model with the provisions in the

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(4) Covered Entities shall document the model methodology, model specifications,
validation reports and back-testing methodology and results.
enriched_requirements.object: model specifications, validation reports and back-testing methodology and results

enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
The documentation
shall, inter alia, demonstrate that the model satisfies all model standards
prescribed in these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: that the model satisfies all model standards prescribed in these Directions

conditions: In case of use of a third-party model
enriched_requirements.full_sentence:
(5) In case of use of a third-party model, Covered Entities shall ensure compliance of
the third-party model with the provisions in these Directions.
enriched_requirements.object: third-party model with the provisions in these Directions
